title: Stale read autocommit query will not block GC, the `TxnStart` is 0
description:
## Bug Report

Please answer these questions before submitting your issue. Thanks!

### 1. Minimal reproduce step (Required)

In session 1:

```
create table t(a int);
insert into t values (1);
begin;
set global tidb_external_ts = @@tidb_current_ts;
commit;
set tidb_enable_external_ts_read = ON;

select *, sleep(100) from t;
```

In session 2:

```
select * from information_schema.cluster_processlist;
```

You will find that the `TxnStart` is empty for the session 1.

### 2. What did you expect to see? (Required)

The `TxnStart` is not empty

### 3. What did you see instead (Required)

The `TxnStart` is empty.

### 4. What is your TiDB version? (Required)

```
mysql> select tidb_version();
+----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| tidb_version()                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
+----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Release Version: v8.5.0
Edition: Community
Git Commit Hash: d13e52ed6e22cc5789bed7c64c861578cd2ed55b
Git Branch: HEAD
UTC Build Time: 2024-12-18 02:26:06
GoVersion: go1.23.3
Race Enabled: false
Check Table Before Drop: false
Store: tikv |
+----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
1 row in set (0.01 sec)
```
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t1(id INT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(50), created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
SELECT * FROM t1 WHERE id = (SELECT MAX(id) FROM t1) AND name IS NOT NULL;
key_bug_features: Stale read autocommit query does not block garbage collection
